steps.title: Read circle P
steps.content: A circle in standard form $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$ has center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$. For $(x+4)^2+(y-6)^2=16$, the center is $(-4,6)$ and the radius is $4$.

steps.title: Transform the circle
steps.content: Circle $Q$ is $2$ units right and $3$ units down from $(-4,6)$, so its center is $(-2,3)$. Its diameter is doubled, so its radius is doubled from $4$ to $8$.

steps.title: Write circle Q
steps.content: Use center $(-2,3)$ and radius $8$, so $r^2=64$. $$(x+2)^2+(y-3)^2=64$$ The answer is D.
